description:
A warehouse facility will lose heating during a storm where temperatures will drop to -15 °C. Determine which of the following chemicals are at risk of solidifying (freezing) and bursting their containers by researching their melting/freezing points on CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/): Acetic acid, glacial; Acetone; Nitrobenzene; Toluene; and Phenol. Create a report at '~/Documents/freezing_risk_report.txt' identifying if each chemical solidifies at -15 °C (melting point > -15 °C).

The report must follow this exact format:

CHEMICAL: Acetic acid, glacial | MELTING_POINT_C: <value> | SOLIDIFIES_AT_MINUS15: <YES/NO>
CHEMICAL: Acetone | MELTING_POINT_C: <value> | SOLIDIFIES_AT_MINUS15: <YES/NO>
CHEMICAL: Nitrobenzene | MELTING_POINT_C: <value> | SOLIDIFIES_AT_MINUS15: <YES/NO>
CHEMICAL: Toluene | MELTING_POINT_C: <value> | SOLIDIFIES_AT_MINUS15: <YES/NO>
CHEMICAL: Phenol | MELTING_POINT_C: <value> | SOLIDIFIES_AT_MINUS15: <YES/NO>
TOTAL_AT_RISK: <number>
PRIORITY_MOVE_LIST: <comma-separated names of chemicals that solidify>
